result:
## Summary

### Root Cause

The bug was in `upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe()` in `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ASTNodeExtension.kt:234`.

When `SpacingAroundCurlyRule` detected a missing space before `{` (LBRACE) and called `node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")` on the LBRACE node, the whitespace was inserted **inside** the BLOCK node (as a sibling of LBRACE) rather than **outside** the BLOCK node (between the closing parenthesis and the BLOCK).

This happened because LBRACE is the **first child** of BLOCK, meaning `treePrev` is `null`. The code fell through to `rawInsertBeforeMe()`, which inserts a new node as a sibling within the same parent — placing the whitespace inside BLOCK as `BLOCK(WHITE_SPACE, LBRACE, ...)`.

The other two rules (`function-start-of-body-spacing` and `function-signature`) then couldn't find the whitespace where they expected it (before the BLOCK node), so they reported errors again, creating a cycle that couldn't be resolved in 3 format passes.

### Fix

In `upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe()`, when `treePrev` is `null` (the node is the first child of its parent) and the previous leaf is not whitespace, instead of using `rawInsertBeforeMe` (which inserts within the same parent), we now delegate to `previous.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(text)`. This places the whitespace in the correct position in the AST — after the previous leaf (e.g., RPAR `)`) rather than inside the composite BLOCK node.

This is consistent with how the non-leaf branch of the same function already works (lines 247-254), which delegates to `prevLeaf.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(text)`.

### Changed file

- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ASTNodeExtension.kt:242-243` — Added an `else if (treePrev == null)` branch that delegates to `previous.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(text)` instead of falling through to `rawInsertBeforeMe`.
